skill.name: make-interfaces-feel-better
skill.description: Applies practical UI polish guidance for frontend interfaces, including typography, surfaces, motion, hit areas, shadows, and interaction details.
skill.summary: Improve frontend interface polish with focused design engineering checks.
skill.author: jakubkrehel

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review showed they are false positives from Markdown text and UI code examples. The skill contains guidance files only, with no executable code, network behavior, secret access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across the skill files. Manual review found Markdown inline code, fenced CSS and TSX examples, and design guidance rather than executable command invocation. No true external command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown prose or fenced CSS and TSX examples. No executable scripts, package hooks, command runners, network calls, or secret access were found in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: High and low static blocker patterns were triggered by design prose, file names, CSS color values, and UI examples. Manual review found no cryptographic APIs, hashing logic, system reconnaissance commands, or network reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain UI design terms and examples, not cryptographic or reconnaissance operations. A targeted search found no suspicious injection, exfiltration, environment access, or network request patterns.

content.value_statement: Frontend interfaces often feel unfinished because small visual and motion details are inconsistent. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused review rules for typography, surfaces, animations, hit areas, and performance.
actual_capabilities: Reviews nested border radius, shadows, image outlines, and hit areas for visual consistency.
Guides typography choices such as balanced headings, pretty text wrapping, font smoothing, and tabular numbers.
Recommends interruptible transitions, staggered entry motion, subtle exits, and contextual icon animation values.
Identifies animation performance issues such as broad transitions and unnecessary will-change usage.
Formats UI review output as before-and-after tables grouped by design principle.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not run automated visual tests.
It focuses on interface polish, not complete product strategy or brand identity.
It assumes the target project already has frontend code or UI requirements to review.
It does not replace accessibility, usability, or cross-browser testing.

use_cases.title: Polish A React Component
use_cases.description: Review a component for radius, shadows, motion, typography, and hit area issues before release.

use_cases.title: Tighten A Design System
use_cases.description: Standardize surface treatment, animation behavior, and typography details across reusable components.

use_cases.title: Improve Product UI Reviews
use_cases.description: Turn subjective feedback like feels off into concrete before-and-after implementation changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component with the make-interfaces-feel-better skill. Focus on the most visible polish issues and give before-and-after recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Motion Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the animations in this UI. Check interruptibility, entry and exit timing, icon transitions, scale on press, and page load behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Polish A Design System Surface
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these card, modal, and button styles. Improve border radius, shadows, image outlines, optical alignment, and minimum hit areas.

prompt_templates.title: Perform A Full UI Polish Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review the provided frontend files. Group findings by principle and return a concise table of every change made.

output_examples.input: A card has the same radius on the outer container and inner image, with eight pixels of padding.
output_examples.output: The review recommends a larger outer radius and a smaller inner radius so the nested surfaces look concentric.

output_examples.input: A toolbar icon appears only on hover and switches instantly between two states.
output_examples.output: The review recommends keeping both icons present and cross-fading opacity, scale, and blur for a smoother state change.

output_examples.input: A dashboard counter shifts horizontally as numbers update.
output_examples.output: The review recommends tabular numbers so each digit has equal width and the layout stays stable.

best_practices: Apply the guidance during implementation, then verify the result visually in the running interface.
Use the before-and-after table format so each polish change stays concrete and reviewable.
Combine this skill with accessibility checks for focus states, contrast, keyboard support, and reduced motion.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for user research, accessibility testing, or product requirements.
Do not apply every animation rule blindly when the interface requires a calmer or static experience.
Do not add dependencies only to satisfy an animation suggestion when CSS can solve the interaction.

faq.question: What kind of work is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for polishing frontend components, product screens, and design system details.

faq.question: Does it generate complete UI designs?
faq.answer: No. It reviews and improves interface details in existing or planned frontend work.

faq.question: Can it work with Tailwind and React?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance includes Tailwind, CSS, React, and motion library examples.

faq.question: Does it require Framer Motion?
faq.answer: No. It recommends motion libraries only when the project already uses them.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found Markdown guidance only and no executable security risk.

faq.question: How should reviewers use the output?
faq.answer: Use the grouped before-and-after tables as an implementation checklist for interface polish.
